content.text: Cloud computing has revolutionized how businesses operate, offering scalable resources over the internet. This lesson covers its definition, benefits, and basic components.

Cloud computing is the delivery of computing services — servers, storage, databases, networking, software, and analytics — over the internet ('the cloud'), enabling faster innovation, flexible resources, and economies of scale.
Cost-efficiency: lower total cost, no on-premise hardware
Scalability: adjust resources to match demand
Accessibility: reach services from anywhere online
Disaster recovery: better redundancy and backups
IaaS — Infrastructure as a Service: virtualized computing resources
PaaS — Platform as a Service: hardware and software tools online
SaaS — Software as a Service: applications delivered over the internet
Public cloud: infrastructure offered off-site over the internet
Private cloud: dedicated infrastructure for one organization
Hybrid cloud: a mix of on-premise and cloud resources
Understanding the core concepts of cloud computing is essential for grasping architecture, service integration, and strategic implementation in future lessons.
